title: regression: tls verify should be set on registries.conf if insecure
body:
In the case where podman needs to pull an image, if that registry that the image
resides on is known to be insesure (as defined in /etc/containers/registries.conf),
tls-verify should be altered on the fly.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: I've tested this with a local build and an internal registry that is insecure successfully.  Thank you for the quick resolution.
I don't like the overloading of `--tls-verify` and propose that we add a new flag, `--tls-force`, to control forcing secure pulls

How would you define --tls-verify?  I would say if I indicate that, I expect the pull operation to be verified.  If I specified --tls-verify=false, I would expect the opposite.  If I specify neither I would figure the config file would handle it.

@rhatdan But --tls-verify defaults to true. If it's always true, what's the point of having insecure registries at all? We can say that manually specifying --tls-verify at the command line is different, but now we're trying to get three states out of a boolean flag, so we've made "true" at the command line different from the default "true".

This seems really confusing to me, and probably to users as well.

It is coded as @rhatdan described ... while tls-verify does default to true, the forceSecure bool you suggested determines if the user asked for tls-verify or took the default.  If they took the default and there is an insecure registry match, it will pull it insecure.
@mheon Right.  as @baude stated, there is a difference between a user typing --tls-verify versus not, and that is what "FORCES" tls.

@rhatdan Sure, but we're going to have to explain this to people in a way that's not confusing.

@baude Can we get manpage changes describing how this works?

Sure we should update the man page and the usage text in the command.

--tls-verify 
    If set to true then tls verification will be used,  If set to false then tls verification will not be used.
  If not specified tls verification will be used, unless the target registry is listed as an insecure registry in
registries.conf.
Added an edited version of @rhatdan 's comments.  Now to get tests to pass
